_note: Proposed Layer 2 entry for data/stat_bank.json. Written to the article output folder only; the central process merges it. This subagent does NOT edit data/stat_bank.json directly (per ad-hoc batch instructions).
stat: Orchestration cost premium of a multi-agent orchestrated AI system over a single AI agent
applies_to: clusters: Technical & Build; Tools & emerging vocabulary
method: Recipe D — cost-per-outcome ratio derived from published Anthropic token multipliers

sources_used.title: How we built our multi-agent research system

workings: Anthropic reports a multi-agent system uses about 15x the tokens of a chat, and a single agent uses about 4x the tokens of a chat. Dividing isolates the orchestration premium: 15 / 4 = 3.75x. Illustrative sterling example: a request costing a single agent £100 in model spend runs to roughly £375 for an orchestrated multi-agent version. Caveat: token count is a proxy for model spend and assumes comparable per-token pricing; it is a coordination-cost indicator, not full TCO (excludes engineering, latency, observability).
